summary: FIX4 multi-select shift-click PASSES after iter_60's two-step fix was applied. (a) useCanvasDrag.js startNodeDrag now gates setSelected behind !shiftKey && !ctrlKey && !metaKey at line 107-109 — verified. (b) MindMapCanvas.jsx adds selectedRef at line 506-507 refreshed via useEffect, and the shift-click branch at line 1487 reads selectedRef.current inside the setMultiSelected functional updater — verified. End-to-end test: navigate /app?unlock=mind-mapper67 → click Idea One → Shift+click Idea Two (via keyboard.down('Shift') + mouse.click + keyboard.up('Shift')) → BOTH nodes' getComputedStyle().outline = 'rgb(255, 106, 213) dashed 2px' (magenta dashed). ArrowRight x3 with 60ms delay → dx Idea One = 15.30 px, dx Idea Two = 15.31 px (expected ~15.30 = 3 × 6 logical × 0.85 zoom). Screenshot confirms visible magenta dashed rings around both nodes simultaneously. Idea Three/Four/Five remain unselected (outline 'none'). One residual 'Maximum update depth exceeded' React warning emits once during the test (after ArrowRight presses) — same warning iter_60 flagged. Non-blocking but worth investigating.

ui_bugs.component: MindMapCanvas — React update-depth warning during multi-select
ui_bugs.issue: After click(A) + shift-click(B) + ArrowRight x3, console emits one 'Maximum update depth exceeded' warning. Functionality is correct (both nodes outlined and nudged) but suggests a setState cascade somewhere in the multi-select / arrow-nudge path. Likely a useEffect re-running due to a Set identity change on every render, or the new selectedRef sync useEffect interacting with a downstream effect that depends on `selected`. Consider memoising the multiSelected Set or guarding the selectedRef-update useEffect with a value-equality check.

fix_results.FIX4_multi_select_arrow_x3_after_shift_c: PASS — Both Idea One and Idea Two have outline 'rgb(255, 106, 213) dashed 2px' after click+shift-click. ArrowRight x3 moved both by dx=15.30 / 15.31 (target ~15.30). Idea Three/Four/Five remain unselected. Screenshot visible.
fix_results.useCanvasDrag_modifier_gate_verified: PASS — useCanvasDrag.js line 107-109 wraps setSelected(id) in if(!e.shiftKey && !e.ctrlKey && !e.metaKey).
fix_results.selectedRef_pattern_verified: PASS — MindMapCanvas.jsx line 506-507 declares selectedRef + useEffect; line 1487-1488 reads selectedRef.current inside the setMultiSelected updater for auto-promote.

action_items: OPTIONAL — Investigate residual 'Maximum update depth exceeded' warning. Functionality is correct but the warning indicates a render cascade. Likely candidates: (1) the multiSelected Set creating new identity on every render and being a dep in some downstream useEffect that calls setMultiSelected; (2) selectedRef useEffect interplay. A useMemo on multiSelected or guarding effects with a stable signature would silence it.
critical_code_review_comments: useCanvasDrag.js startNodeDrag modifier gate (line 107-109) is the right pattern — keeps mousedown selection promotion only for plain clicks, lets onClick own additive multi-select.

MindMapCanvas.jsx selectedRef pattern (line 506-507) mirrors the existing mapRef pattern (line 501-502) cleanly. Both refs are read inside event-handler functional updaters to dodge stale-closure values across rapid mousedown→click→keypress sequences.
Residual 'Maximum update depth exceeded' warning is the only remaining smell — non-blocking but should be tracked.

success_rate.frontend: FIX4 multi-select PASS (1/1 named scenario). Residual non-blocking React warning.

seed_data_creation: None — used the seeded 'Map Title' demo map (Idea One..Five).
context_for_next_testing_agent: FIX4 multi-select is verified PASS as of iter_61 with the two-step fix (modifier-gated setSelected in useCanvasDrag.js + selectedRef in MindMapCanvas.jsx). The setup script that worked: navigate to /app?unlock=mind-mapper67 (no need to dismiss tour — overlapping dialog is centred and does not block node clicks at the corners of the canvas where Idea One/Idea Two sit). Use locator.click(force=True) for the first plain click on Idea One, then page.keyboard.down('Shift') + page.mouse.click(centerX, centerY) + page.keyboard.up('Shift') for the modifier click on Idea Two. Read outlines via getComputedStyle. The 'Maximum update depth exceeded' warning still emits once after ArrowRight x3 — non-blocking, flagged as MEDIUM follow-up.

rca_of_the_issue.symptom_resolved: Previously (iter_59/60), shift-clicking Idea Two after clicking Idea One produced an empty multiSelected Set and outline='none' on both nodes. ArrowRight only moved the second-clicked node.
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: useCanvasDrag.startNodeDrag (mousedown, fires before onClick) unconditionally called setSelected(id) on every node mousedown — including modifier-clicks. This raced the onClick handler's setMultiSelected functional updater whose closure either still saw the OLD selected (and the auto-promote then failed because the actual `selected` had been overwritten by the time the next render committed) or saw the NEW selected (and added only B). Either way the multi-set ended up wrong.
rca_of_the_issue.applied_fix: (a) Modifier-gate setSelected in useCanvasDrag.js startNodeDrag — modifier-clicks no longer overwrite `selected` from mousedown. (b) selectedRef in MindMapCanvas.jsx, refreshed via useEffect on `selected`, read inside the setMultiSelected updater so the auto-promote always sees the LATEST committed selection regardless of closure age.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: click(IdeaOne) + shift-click(IdeaTwo) → both outlines 'rgb(255, 106, 213) dashed 2px'. ArrowRight x3 → both nodes nudge 15.30/15.31 px (3 × 6 × 0.85). Idea Three/Four/Five untouched.
